title: Commit To Pipeline Stage Gating
description: A build-and-CI governance workflow that enforces stage gates, capacity policies, and quality/security controls before pipeline advancement. Focus case: commit to pipeline stage gating.
assumptions: All availability predicates accurately reflect current resource/actor availability and are updated atomically at reservation and release.
Change requests are registered (ingested) before any resource reservation or stage execution is attempted.
Policies, test suites, and scan jobs are idempotent and safe to bind/unbind without side effects beyond reservation semantics.
Human reviewer and approver assignments are represented as discrete tokens that must be consumed and released.
risks: Resource starvation: long-held reservations may block other change requests and create pipeline bottlenecks if not reclaimed.
Stale state risk: failures in release paths could leave resources unavailable (lost tokens), requiring manual remediation.
Human approval latency: manual approvers introduce non-deterministic delays that can slow pipeline throughput.
Inconsistent bindings: misconfiguration of policy or dependency mappings could allow promotion without required checks, undermining governance.
Scale mismatch: finite pools of specialized CI tool instances and infrastructure resources can limit parallelism and increase queuing.
